pi_items.source: Task description requires creating a workbook named 'portfolio_analysis', and '.dva' is the standard Oracle Analytics Desktop extension.
Task description explicitly requires building on a canvas named 'Portfolio Analysis'.
Task description explicitly requires the visualization to be titled 'Product Sub-Category Portfolio Analysis'.
Task description explicitly specifies this path, and the 'setup_oracle_analytics.ps1' script copies the data file to this exact location.
The task description does not specify an output directory. The 'export_result.ps1' script that would typically enforce this path is completely missing from the task environment.
Python script analysis of the 'sample_order_lines2023.xlsx' data reveals exactly 17 distinct 'Product Sub Category' values, satisfying the requirement of at least 10 bubbles.
Python script analysis of the 'sample_order_lines2023.xlsx' data reveals exactly 3 distinct 'Product Category' values, matching the expected distinct colors.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating an Oracle Analytics workbook named 'portfolio_analysis.dva' using the synthetic 'sample_order_lines2023.xlsx' dataset. The dataset correctly contains 3 unique 'Product Category' values (corresponding to distinct colors) and 17 unique 'Product Sub Category' values (corresponding to the bubbles), which satisfies the metadata claim of at least 10 bubbles. A critical defect in this task is that the required hook scripts ('setup_task.ps1' and 'export_result.ps1') are completely missing from the environment, making the task programmatic file verification impossible to pass.
